definitionWarning.notes: Repeating event actions is not a protocol violation but is unintentional in most scenarios.
Repeating status values is not a protocol violation but is unintentional in most scenarios.
Repeating event actions is not a protocol violation but is unintentional in most scenarios.

definitionIgnore.notes: RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the links structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with future RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the notices and remarks structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with future RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the events structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with future RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the public IDs structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with future RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the asEventActor structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the variants structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the variant details structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the secureDNS structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the dsData structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the keyData structure, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 only requires the errorCode in an error response.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the domain object class, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the entity object class, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the nameserver object class, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the help response, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.
RFC 9083 does not prohibit extensions from adding information to the nameserver search response, therefore this test may be incompatible with futurue RDAP extensions.

definitionNotes: This conformance configuration is typical of gTLD RDAP server needs.
